tasks.spec:
You are a Ringer worker implementing ONE new file in a git worktree of the ringer repo. Your current working directory IS that worktree — edit files here directly, leave ALL changes uncommitted (never run git commit; the check exports your diff as a patch), and never touch anything outside this directory. You own EXACTLY ONE file: scripts/feeder_enrich.py (new). Do not modify any other file.

GOAL: a post-run enrichment tool that joins a Ringer run's workers to the Feeder router's request telemetry and writes a summary block into the run's state JSON, so the dashboard can show which concrete models served each worker.

CONTEXT: Ringer workers call LLMs through the Feeder proxy. Each worker attempt writes a log at <workdir>/<task_key>/worker.log containing JSON event lines with a field "sessionID":"ses_..." (one stable id per attempt, possibly repeated on every line). Feeder logs every request row keyed by that session id.

IMPLEMENT scripts/feeder_enrich.py (Python 3.11+, STDLIB ONLY, executable with python3):
CLI: python3 scripts/feeder_enrich.py --state STATE_JSON --workdir WORKDIR [--fixture ROWS_JSON] [--feeder-base URL]
1. Load STATE_JSON (a Ringer run state: object with "tasks": [ {"key": ..., ...}, ... ]).
2. For each task: read WORKDIR/<key>/worker.log; extract session ids by scanning for the literal pattern "sessionID":"<id>" where <id> starts with ses_; dedupe, preserve first-seen order. If the log is missing or has no ids, print a note naming the task and skip it (not an error).
3. Fetch that session's request rows: in FIXTURE mode (--fixture given) read ROWS_JSON, an object mapping session_id -> [row, ...]; in LIVE mode GET {feeder-base}/api/requests?session_id=<id> (feeder-base default http://localhost:3001), accepting either a bare JSON list or {"requests": [...]}; on any HTTP/network error print a note and skip that task, never crash. Each row has: platform, model_id, status ("success" or an error string like "429"), output_tokens, input_tokens, latency_ms, created_at (ISO string).
4. Aggregate per task across all its sessions, rows sorted by created_at when present:
   - sessions: the ordered session id list
   - served: SUCCESS rows only, grouped by (platform, model_id) in first-seen chronological order, each entry exactly {"platform": ..., "model_id": ..., "calls": <count>, "output_tokens": <sum>}
   - failovers: number of transitions between CONSECUTIVE success rows whose (platform, model_id) differ
   - mixed_models: true iff len(served) > 1
   - requests: total row count (all statuses); errors_429: count of rows whose status is "429" (or int 429)
   - latency_ms_total: sum of latency_ms over ALL rows; latency_ms_p50: statistics.median of latency_ms over ALL rows
5. Write the aggregate as task["feeder"] back into STATE_JSON (in place, atomic: write a temp file then os.replace). Also set a top-level state["feeder_enriched_at"] ISO-8601 UTC timestamp. Preserve every other field byte-for-byte semantically (normal json round-trip is fine).
6. Exit 0 when the state was processed (even if some tasks were skipped); exit 1 for unusable arguments or unreadable state. Print one summary line per task saying what was written or why it was skipped.

HOW TO VERIFY (this exact command is the task's check — run it yourself from the worktree root until it passes): sh /home/ajo/ringer/scripts/checks/phase4_enrich_check.sh
The check builds its own fixture (2 DeepSeek successes, one 429, one Mistral success) and asserts the exact aggregate: served=[sambanova/DeepSeek-V3.1 calls 2 tok 38, nvidia/mistral-large-3 calls 1 tok 2], failovers=1, mixed_models=true, requests=4, errors_429=1, latency_ms_total=2270, latency_ms_p50=610.

HARD RULES: stdlib only; no git commit; no network calls in fixture mode; only scripts/feeder_enrich.py may be created or changed; write nothing outside the worktree.
tasks.verified: the enrichment script was executed against a fixture and produced the exact agreed aggregation (models, failovers, tokens, latency) written back into a run state JSON

tasks.spec:
You are a Ringer worker editing ONE existing file in a git worktree of the ringer repo. Your current working directory IS that worktree — edit files here directly, leave ALL changes uncommitted (never run git commit; the check exports your diff as a patch), and never touch anything outside this directory. You own EXACTLY ONE file: ringer.py (large, ~9100 lines — make a small, surgical change). Do not modify any other file.

GOAL: ringer's HTML report pages currently show only the manifest's model string (e.g. feeder/auto/coding). A separate tool now writes real routing telemetry into the run state as an optional per-task dict task["feeder"], shaped like: {"sessions": ["ses_x"], "served": [{"platform": "sambanova", "model_id": "DeepSeek-V3.1", "calls": 2, "output_tokens": 38}, {"platform": "nvidia", "model_id": "mistral-large-3", "calls": 1, "output_tokens": 2}], "failovers": 1, "mixed_models": true, "requests": 4, "errors_429": 1, "latency_ms_total": 2270, "latency_ms_p50": 610}. Render it.

IMPLEMENT: when (and only when) a task dict carries a non-empty "feeder" dict, the python-rendered report pages must show ONE compact telemetry strip for that task, of the form: Served by: sambanova/DeepSeek-V3.1 (2 calls, 38 tok) \u2192 nvidia/mistral-large-3 (1 call, 2 tok) \u00b7 1 failover \u00b7 p50 610ms — models joined with \u2192 in list order, failover count shown only when > 0, p50 shown when present. Add it in BOTH python renderers that show per-task detail: the final report page (module-level function render_final_report_html and its helpers, around lines 3283-3624) and the live status page (render_status_html path), by writing one small shared helper function (e.g. render_feeder_strip(task) -> str returning '' when absent) and calling it from both task-rendering paths. Use the existing html_escape() on every value you interpolate; match the surrounding f-string template style and the existing CSS class conventions (add a minimal inline-safe class like feeder-strip if you add CSS, in the same style blocks the page already uses).

FAIL-OPEN IS MANDATORY: a missing, empty, or malformed feeder block (wrong types, absent keys) must never raise and must render exactly nothing — defensive .get() with defaults throughout the helper. Tasks without the block must render byte-identically to today.

HOW TO VERIFY (this exact command is the task's check — run it yourself from the worktree root until it passes): sh /home/ajo/ringer/scripts/checks/phase4_render_check.sh
The check compiles ringer.py, then renders a two-task state (alpha WITH the feeder block above, bravo WITHOUT) via ringer.render_final_report_html(state, renderer=None, page_path=None) and asserts: the string 'Served by' appears EXACTLY once, both model names appear, and nothing raises.

HARD RULES: only ringer.py may be changed; no git commit; preserve ringer's baked-in invariants (stdin closed, explicit sandbox flags, executed verification, raw logs — do not touch worker-invocation code at all); keep the change minimal and localized to rendering.
tasks.verified: ringer.py compiles and the rendered report shows the Served-by telemetry strip exactly once for the task that has telemetry, with fail-open behavior proven for the task that lacks it
